115748.explanation: A referendum gives residents more worries that we actually want to spare them. Participation is a much better alternative.

115749.explanation: A public garden can be realized with agreements; a sports hall is somewhat more difficult because the responsibility is greater.

115751.explanation: Absolutely good for earning some extra money and you can also teach people something about farming. However, it must not harm agriculture.

115754.explanation: We agree with this statement because we do not want any more shelter in the future. We believe we have already done enough at this time.

115756.explanation: We have excellent places in our municipality where seasonal workers have lived for years. This works well.

115757.explanation: In principle yes, but regular logging in forest areas is already allowed anyway, so we cannot judge that. In neighborhoods, you have to look at each tree, each situation.

115760.explanation: This is probably not affordable and also not feasible with the police.

115762.explanation: The current standards are fine and must be enforced.

115766.explanation: The local broadcaster (RTV Horizon) already receives subsidies.

115768.explanation: The priority of the school lies with the basic curriculum. Music education is optional.

115769.explanation: Financial aid is not always the solution; the municipality can also facilitate in other ways.

115773.explanation: We want to enhance safety so we agree. However, it is important that our residents also experience it that way and do not feel these measures are used to watch them.

115774.explanation: We do want to investigate if there is room for this and if safety can be guaranteed.

115775.explanation: Of course, it would be fantastic if we could realize that.

115777.explanation: We do not want campgrounds to be used for permanent housing, nor do we want extra residential neighborhoods to emerge in rural areas.

115778.explanation: We want to preserve the rural area for nature and agriculture.

115780.explanation: We want everyone to be able to continue participating.

115781.explanation: It should not be about income but about how much you have available to spend net each month.

115783.explanation: It should not come at the expense of regular parking spaces. New charging points are welcome.

115784.explanation: Residential areas and centers are already 30 km/h zones, but major access roads should not be 30 km/h.

115785.explanation: This is not the responsibility of the municipality.

115787.explanation: We must remain realistic here and we are dealing with the CPI index for increases.

115789.explanation: Yes, with an increase we can do nice things like renovating bike paths which are good for tourism.

115790.explanation: Small expansions of current industrial areas are welcome. Also look at organizing what we have to create opportunities for starters.

115792.explanation: We want priority for our own residents.

115794.explanation: This must be possible via rooftops. There must be a good energy plan for the entire municipality.

115798.explanation: Haha, fun! Completely agree, dialect is good for learning other languages later in life.

115807.explanation: Attention should go to the clubs in Leende and Sterksel. Good facilities must be made available there.

115748.explanation: A referendum is a serious measure. I answered 'agree' because the word important precedes it. AbHL wishes to involve residents on topics suitable for it. This does not always have to be a referendum.

115749.explanation: This is a right that residents have.

115751.explanation: This is a customized approach weighing multiple interests.

115756.explanation: That is possible, but not always necessary.

115757.explanation: AbHL wants the municipality of Heeze-Leende to be the green lung for the urban area where people like to recreate.

115760.explanation: If there are initiators who want to take this up, the municipality can facilitate it.

115762.explanation: We must set the maximum standards so that no hearing damage occurs.

115766.explanation: We already support the local broadcaster.

115768.explanation: AbHL wants to give children a healthy foundation. For that, it is necessary that they can participate.

115769.explanation: The responsibility basically lies with the association itself. AbHL cherishes community life. Sometimes that requires financial support.

115773.explanation: We already have a lot of camera surveillance.

115774.explanation: This should be arranged regionally, and then it is more logical to open a women's shelter centrally (urban-wise).

115775.explanation: There are increasingly more single-person households that want a small home.

115777.explanation: Holiday homes are needed in a recreational municipality. In case of high housing shortage, this must be tolerated.

115778.explanation: Now that De Bulders is almost fully built, we must look for expansion plans for seven years from now and beyond.

115780.explanation: There are clear rules residents must meet to be eligible for this, and these rules must be maintained.

115781.explanation: It is already higher than legally required. We must try to keep this at the average of comparable municipalities.

115783.explanation: AbHL wants the municipality to respond to the need in this matter.

115784.explanation: Research shows that areas where the speed is limited to 30 km/h have far fewer serious injury accidents. The road network must then be designed for 30 km/h.

115785.explanation: A healthcare provider must provide good care.

115787.explanation: With taxes and benefits, AbHL wants to aim for the average in comparable municipalities.

115789.explanation: AbHL wants to keep taxes in line with the average of comparable municipalities.

115790.explanation: Space must be made for expansion. Not for heavy industry.

115792.explanation: The housing shortage is so great that even local residents need housing. We can accommodate refugees with residence status assigned to Heeze-Leende by providing shelter through room rental.

115794.explanation: We choose an energy transition without wind turbines and large-scale solar farms. We focus on green gas, solar panels on roofs, and making homes more sustainable.

115798.explanation: That is up to the schools themselves.

115802.explanation: That helps to ease the housing shortage.

115807.explanation: In consultation with the Brabantsedag, the municipality must look for the most suitable solutions. The wishes of the Brabantsedag weigh heavily here.

115748.explanation: Participation is an important part of arriving at good decision-making. An advisory referendum is one instrument for that.

115749.explanation: Right to challenge is an important part of citizen participation.

115751.explanation: Farmers must have perspective, but the countryside must also remain recognized as countryside.

115754.explanation: We have a shared responsibility in this.

115756.explanation: The responsibility for housing lies with employers, but this does not necessarily have to be on their own premises.

115757.explanation: This must be considered on a case-by-case basis.

115760.explanation: We do not see that as the municipality's task to organize, and there are other priorities where the money can be spent.

115762.explanation: This is already well established in the General Local Regulation (APV).

115766.explanation: Local broadcasters are essential in providing information to residents.

115768.explanation: There is already a scheme if these children are members of the fanfare.

115769.explanation: (Sports) clubs form the basis of a healthy society where people do something active together.

115773.explanation: Camera surveillance is of little use if there are insufficient legal possibilities to use the footage.

115774.explanation: This is not appropriate in our municipality.

115775.explanation: The municipality must take good care of its own residents.

115777.explanation: It is currently tolerated and can provide a temporary solution for the tight housing market. However, these homes must meet legal requirements.

115778.explanation: There is still enough space for infill development.

115780.explanation: These burdens should be borne by those with the broadest shoulders.

115781.explanation: Income policy is national policy. This should not differ per municipality.

115783.explanation: Parking in the neighborhoods is generally already a problem in Heeze-Leende. It is important that there is sufficient parking, with or without charging points.

115784.explanation: This is not possible because important connecting roads are located within the built-up area.

115785.explanation: The municipality has a facilitating role, not a determining one.

115790.explanation: More attention must be paid to the suitability of businesses on the industrial sites.

115792.explanation: The municipality must meet the government’s targets but should also ensure sufficient housing is built for its own residents.

115794.explanation: We are not in favor of panels directly on the land itself, but we do support them on the roofs of farms.

115798.explanation: Educational institutions within the municipality are responsible for their curriculum, but we consider the Brabant dialect an important asset that should be preserved.

115802.explanation: This causes extra administrative pressure in monitoring the proper living conditions. Sufficient houses must be built to address the housing shortage.

115807.explanation: The Brabant Day is an important part of community life in our municipality. The carnival clubs in Leende should also be able to receive these facilities.

115748.explanation: Input from residents on important issues is very important. We do this informally through personal contacts and formally through participation procedures. Organizing a referendum is complex and costly and is not our preference.

115749.explanation: Depending on the function; hospitality could be allowed and maintenance of public gardens as well, provided conclusive agreements are made with the municipal council.

115751.explanation: Farmers must be able to sustainably earn a living with their business. Moreover, they ensure quality and experience in the countryside. If possible, we support initiatives. They must not come at the expense of nature, our village centers, or the operations of other (farm) businesses.

115754.explanation: The municipality is legally obliged to arrange asylum accommodation. We do this on a small scale under our own management, which causes hardly any nuisance.

115756.explanation: With the current shortage of housing for our own residents, it is not desirable that regular homes are used for seasonal workers. We cooperate in providing housing on their own premises. In any case, this occurs only very limitedly in our municipality.

115757.explanation: Trees are of great importance for people and nature. Cutting down trees should be possible but must be compensated. This cannot always be done at the same location. Therefore, compensation elsewhere within the municipality is permitted.

115760.explanation: Our municipality does not have sufficient resources to organize a meaningful fireworks display. There are better regional opportunities for this.

115762.explanation: Events are very important for liveliness, retaining youth, and social interaction. Nuisance should be limited through good consultation and good organization, not through increasingly strict rules.

115766.explanation: The local broadcaster already receives financial support from the municipality. Additional funds are not necessary or justifiable in our opinion.

115768.explanation: Making and listening to music together connects people. To ensure this for the future, music lessons for youth are necessary. The municipality has contracts with the music school for this purpose.

115769.explanation: For many sports, the municipality makes a large contribution to the facilities. For the rest, the association itself is responsible. We cannot pass the costs of declining associations on to other residents.

115773.explanation: Camera surveillance provides a feeling of security and makes detection and prosecution simpler in case of incidents - which also has a preventive effect.

115774.explanation: Our municipality is too small to organize this independently. Therefore, we are in favor of a regional facility.

115775.explanation: It is very important that our residents can find suitable housing in their own municipality. Given the current tight housing market, priority is an appropriate measure.

115777.explanation: Holiday homes are intended for recreational users and tourists and should remain so. In special situations, temporary stay in a holiday home can be the only option, and then we allow this to a limited extent.

115778.explanation: In our rapidly growing region, all additional homes cannot be realized within existing village boundaries, and sometimes expansion will be necessary.

115780.explanation: In principle, every resident should contribute to the costs incurred by the municipality. Automatically waiving taxes for everyone on benefits is therefore not our preference – this should be tailored, looking beyond income alone.

115781.explanation: Help with poverty should always be tailored and not bound to strict limits, according to the VVD. Strict limits lead on one hand to misuse and on the other to distressing cases.

115783.explanation: Public charging points should not be used as permanent parking spots for electric cars. Currently, there are enough places available for charging cars. If the electric fleet grows, more spots can be added.

115784.explanation: Lower speeds ensure higher safety and less nuisance. A number of roads still need to be adjusted for this. The bypass should remain at 50 km/h to encourage through traffic to use it.

115785.explanation: Care primarily concerns quality, availability, and affordability – the government plays a role in this. If someone has more specific requirements, these do not have to be arranged nearby.

115787.explanation: The municipality must remain critical of the burdens on our residents and reduce costs where possible. The possibilities for this are determined by the wishes regarding municipal services and facilities.

115789.explanation: Tourists also make use of our facilities and infrastructure and must contribute to the costs – we cannot shift all expenses onto our own residents.

115790.explanation: In our municipality, many people want to start or expand businesses. This creates employment and a variety of services for our residents. Moreover, local entrepreneurs support associations and events.

115792.explanation: People with a residence permit generally have the same legal rights and obligations as native Dutch citizens. For the VVD, this includes equal waiting times for housing as well as equal rights to emergency regulations when applicable.

115794.explanation: Good agricultural land must remain available for farming, and solar parks should not drive up the price of agricultural land. However, there is also poor-quality agricultural land that is out of sight, where a solar park could fit well.

115798.explanation: Teaching dialect is not a primary function of education. Furthermore, dialect is a local matter – in our view, 'Brabants' does not exist.

115802.explanation: Residents should be able to rent out one or two rooms in their own homes and forms of assisted living should be possible. Unrestricted commercial room rentals increase the chance of nuisance and exploitation and must remain prohibited.

115803.explanation: For hospitality businesses, shopkeepers, and residents, it is important that there is sufficient parking in the center. With the opening of the new parking lot at Albert Heijn, we believe this is currently adequately addressed.

115807.explanation: The Brabantsedag in Heeze and carnival in Leende are part of our cultural heritage and important events in our municipality. We must do everything to preserve them. Creating good construction sites is a joint task for the municipality and building groups.

115748.explanation: A referendum can ensure that voters actively participate in the public debate on matters of common interest. By allowing them to form opinions on such issues themselves, citizens are encouraged to engage and take part in the discussion.

115749.explanation: Our preference is that management remains with the municipality.

115751.explanation: It must be possible to start a different business on a farm, such as a campsite or shop.

115754.explanation: Reception of asylum seekers will remain a responsibility for all municipalities.

115756.explanation: Less pressure on the housing market. It significantly reduces commuting. Social control. Disadvantages: mixing work and private life; high dependency.

115757.explanation: It depends on the location of the tree, its size, and the rules of your specific municipality. Sometimes a tree is so diseased or dangerous that it must be removed.

115760.explanation: A professional fireworks show offers several advantages over setting off consumer fireworks yourself. A central show brings neighborhood residents together in one place.

115762.explanation: Noise standards do not need to be stricter. They must be properly enforced. No nuisance.

115768.explanation: Music education is important in the development of children.

115769.explanation: It is important to know what caused the shortage.

115773.explanation: Advantage: Prevention and deterrence. Disadvantage: Infringement on privacy. Advantage: Subjective safety. Disadvantage: Crime shifts to the next street where no camera is installed.

115774.explanation: Sufficient availability is present in the region.

115775.explanation: Social cohesion, informal care, and family ties.

115778.explanation: Here too, we take every opportunity (review/investigate) to make housing construction possible.

115780.explanation: The possibility of debt forgiveness must remain.

115783.explanation: Increase in these vehicles calls for expansion of charging points.

115785.explanation: Care provided by companies that are available.

115789.explanation: Tourists are important. Tourist tax should not be increased.

115790.explanation: There are already sufficient business parks.

115798.explanation: Fine if schools take this up, but not directed by the municipality.

115802.explanation: Renting out can (partly) solve the housing problem.

115803.explanation: Supporter of creating more parking spaces.

115807.explanation: This also applies to the building groups for the carnival parade in Leende.

115748.explanation: Agree. We find it important to experiment with new forms of citizen participation such as, for example, a citizen’s assembly. Child participation is also highly valued by us.

115749.explanation: Agree. This already happens in Heeze – Leende (for example at the sports fields at the Lambrek).

115751.explanation: Disagree, due to the word 'always' in this statement. We are very positive about this, but it must fit the environment. Therefore, assessment by the municipality is necessary.

115754.explanation: Disagree, in the coming period we want to continue fulfilling our responsibility towards this vulnerable group of refugees. In line with the dispersal law.

115756.explanation: Agree, primarily this is the responsibility of employers. The municipality does have a role in monitoring the quality of housing.

115757.explanation: Disagree, more is needed than just a new tree. We would like to include an obligation to compensate with one or more trees with a comparable canopy area when trees are cut down.

115760.explanation: Agree, aligns with the measures regarding the national fireworks ban that will come into effect at the turn of 2026/2027.

115762.explanation: Agree, recently (autumn 2025) the General Police Regulation (APV) was updated tightening the noise standards. We agree with this.

115766.explanation: Agree, where needed we want to support the local broadcaster (in our case RTV Horizon) in their independent reporting.

115768.explanation: Agree, this is already possible within the municipality of Heeze-Leende. The age limit is 18 years.

115769.explanation: Agree, but based on a number of strict criteria such as number of members and future viability. The principle is equal treatment for all.

115773.explanation: Agree, where necessary due to issues like nuisance or traffic safety we are open to this. Clearly within the boundaries of privacy legislation.

115774.explanation: Disagree, such facilities should be organized regionally in Southeast Brabant with the highest possible security guarantees. The location must of course remain secret.

115775.explanation: Disagree, we have an agreed system in Southeast Brabant that we fully support. See wooniezie.nl. However, customization per municipality should be possible via, for example, a housing ordinance.

115777.explanation: Disagree, this is not in line with current agreements within our municipality which we support.

115778.explanation: Agree, we want to build as much as possible within the existing village boundaries. Special attention to new small-scale neighborhoods in Leende and Sterksel to ensure sufficient support for a good level of facilities in these villages.

115780.explanation: Disagree, we consider this one of the important elements of the minimum income policy.

115781.explanation: Agree, in addition to raising the income threshold, it’s also important to improve accessibility and reach of minimum schemes so that people who need such arrangements are effectively helped.

115783.explanation: Agree, this is to encourage this sustainable mode of transport (less CO2). Coordinated with the availability of electric cars.

115784.explanation: Agree, we want public spaces to be designed more for pedestrians and cyclists, for safety, comfort, environment, and health. This includes having a maximum speed of 30 km/h within built-up areas.

115785.explanation: Agree, but we also want to limit the number of different care providers in a neighborhood and establish a fixed care and support team per neighborhood.

115787.explanation: Disagree, we want to use property tax (OZB) where necessary to increase municipal income and thereby maintain facilities. Especially in times of budget cuts from the national government. The strongest shoulders must carry the heaviest burdens.

115789.explanation: Agree, the Heeze-Leende council recently (autumn 2025) decided to raise tourist tax specifically on more expensive hotel stays. We agree with this.

115790.explanation: Disagree, we are open to intensifying business parks if needed to make room for more businesses. We do not support expanding business parks. In addition, we want to restructure existing parks and improve their quality.

115792.explanation: Agree, while realizing that national regulations apply here. Furthermore, we want to create rental housing for temporary stays for people in acute situations (such as residents in divorce, youth, and status holders) as a base to look for permanent housing.

115794.explanation: Disagree, our primary focus is on solar panels on roofs, but if a solar park is well integrated into its surroundings and a connection is available to the electricity grid so that the electricity can actually be used, we are open to this.

115798.explanation: Agree, as part of culture lessons we can support this.

115802.explanation: Agree, certainly contributes to the demand for housing, for example for young people.

115803.explanation: Disagree, we want fewer cars and fewer parking spots in the center, especially along Kapelstraat and Jan Deckerstraat. This is possible now that a large parking lot has been added near the new AH, making the center more attractive for cyclists and pedestrians.

115807.explanation: Disagree, the builders themselves have expressed a preference to continue building in tents at the current locations. However, we want to respond to the groups' request to better facilitate them with basic amenities at the construction site (such as toilet, sewer connection, and internet).

statements.title: The municipality must provide space to the Brabantsedag building groups, for example in a shed or warehouse, so they can work on their float.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The residents of Heeze-Leende must be able to give their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Heeze-Leende moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
For every tree cut down in the municipality, a new one must be planted.
The asylum reception center in our municipality should be closed as soon as possible.

titleParts.glossary: A place where people who have applied for asylum are accommodated. An asylum shelter can be temporary. A permanent asylum shelter is called an asylum seekers' centre (azc).

titleParts.text: in onze gemeente moet zo snel mogelijk sluiten.

statements.title: Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.
More camera surveillance must be introduced in Heeze-Leende.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
A women's shelter (domestic violence refuge) must be established in Heeze-Leende.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The municipality must make it possible for all primary schools to include attention to the Brabant dialect.
The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
More business parks must be developed in Heeze-Leende.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: moeten woningzoekenden uit de eigen gemeente voorrang krijgen.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: The municipality should allow landlords to rent out individual rooms instead of entire houses.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: More parking spaces must be created in the center of Heeze.
